# Corrected distributional summary (auto-generated)

> Generated by `scripts/reconcile_claims.py` from data already on disk. No models are run. Paper Table 1 basis: `broader_analysis_results.json` (first 8 layers, 2-way Laplace/Gaussian). Columns below use `layerwise_model_comparison.json` (all layers, 4-way BIC fit).

| Model | Family | Layers | Paper Lap% (2-way, 8L) | BIC winners (4-way, all layers) | Plain-Laplace BIC wins | Median GGD β | Regime |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| gpt2 | GPT | 12 | 62.5% | GGD 3, Student-t 9 | 0 | 1.537 | intermediate, near-Gaussian |
| gpt2-medium | GPT | 15 | 75.0% | GGD 1, Student-t 14 | 0 | 1.554 | intermediate, near-Gaussian |
| gpt2-large | GPT | 15 | 100.0% | GGD 9, Student-t 6 | 0 | 1.310 | Laplacian-to-intermediate |
| bert-base-uncased | BERT | 12 | 12.5% | Student-t 12 | 0 | 1.689 | intermediate, near-Gaussian |
| bert-large-uncased | BERT | 15 | 0.0% | Student-t 13, GGD 2 | 0 | 1.675 | intermediate, near-Gaussian |
| distilbert-base-uncased | BERT | 6 | 16.7% | Student-t 6 | 0 | 1.740 | intermediate, near-Gaussian |
| roberta-base | RoBERTa | 12 | 100.0% | GGD 12 | 0 | 1.353 | Laplacian-to-intermediate |
| roberta-large | RoBERTa | 15 | 37.5% | Student-t 14, GGD 1 | 0 | 1.546 | intermediate, near-Gaussian |
| distilroberta-base | RoBERTa | 6 | 83.3% | GGD 5, Student-t 1 | 0 | 1.322 | Laplacian-to-intermediate |
| albert-base-v2 | BERT | 1 | 100.0% | GGD 1 | 0 | 1.395 | Laplacian-to-intermediate |
| google/electra-small-discriminator | ELECTRA | 12 | 62.5% | Student-t 7, GGD 5 | 0 | 1.411 | Laplacian-to-intermediate |
| facebook/bart-base | EncDec | 6 | 100.0% | GGD 6 | 0 | 1.373 | Laplacian-to-intermediate |
| t5-small | EncDec | 6 | 100.0% | GGD 6 | 0 | 0.586 | sub-Laplacian (very peaked) |
| t5-base | EncDec | 12 | 100.0% | GGD 12 | 0 | 0.579 | sub-Laplacian (very peaked) |
| google/mt5-small | EncDec | 8 | 100.0% | GGD 8 | 0 | 0.537 | sub-Laplacian (very peaked) |

## Headline reconciliation

- **Layers best-fit by plain Laplace under BIC, across all 15 models: 0.** The paper's central noun is "Laplace"; under the 4-way BIC fit it wins zero layers. Winners are the generalized Gaussian (GGD) or Student-t.
- **Models with median GGD β < 1 (sharper than Laplace, most compressible): ['t5-small', 't5-base', 'google/mt5-small'].** These encoder-decoder models — not the GPT family — carry the real quantization/pruning signal.
- **"BERT stays Gaussian" does not hold under the 4-way fit.** BERT-family layers are predominantly best-fit by Student-t (heavy-tailed), not Gaussian.
